Subtitles.Longitude: 2.1 Location calculated by the European Environment Agency
Subtitles.3.1.a: 3.1 Habitat types of Annex I of Council Directive 92/43/EEC present on the site
Subtitles.3.2.a: 3.2 Species referred to in Article 4 of Directive 2009/147/EC and species listed in Annex II to Directive 92/43/EEC present on the site
Subtitles.5.1.1: 5.1 Body responsible for the site management

F_2_3_biogeographical_regions.F_2_3_3_marine_relationship_biogeo: Fully marine SCI/SAC (100 %) must be attributed to a biogeographical region

HabitatClasses.N02: Tidal rivers, Estuaries, Mud flats, Sand flats, Lagoons (including saltwork basins)
HabitatClasses.N03: Salt marshes, Salt pastures, Salt steppes
HabitatClasses.N04: Coastal sand dunes, Sand beaches, Machair
HabitatClasses.N06: Inland water bodies (Standing water, Running water)
HabitatClasses.N07: Bogs, Marshes, Water fringed vegetation, Fens
HabitatClasses.N08: Heath, Scrub, Maquis and Garrigue, Phygrana
HabitatClasses.N12: Extensive cereal cultures (including Rotation cultures with regular fallowing)
HabitatClasses.N20: Artificial forest monoculture (e.g. Plantations of poplar or Exotic trees)
HabitatClasses.N21: Non-forest areas cultivated with woody plants (including Orchards, groves, Vineyards, Dehesas)
HabitatClasses.N22: Inland rocks, Screes, Sands, Permanent Snow and ice
HabitatClasses.N23: Other land (including Towns, Villages, Roads, Waste places, Mines, Industrial sites)

F_3_1_a_essential_information.Priority form: For habitat types 6210, 7130, 9430 priority depends on the habitat characteristics. Indicated as Yes/No.
F_3_1_a_essential_information.Not present: Indicates whether the habitat type is no longer present (noLongerPresent) on the site, or its re-establishment is planned (reEstablishment).
F_3_1_a_essential_information.Cover: Shows the size of habitat type as decimal values.
F_3_1_a_essential_information.Caves: For habitat types 8310 and 8330 (caves), the number of caves can be entered when a surface area is not available or appropriate.
F_3_1_a_essential_information.Method cover: Method used for cover: complete = complete survey or a statistically robust estimate; limited = based mainly on extrapolation from a limited amount of data; insufficient = insufficient or no data available
F_3_1_a_essential_information.Last data collection: Date or period of the last data collection.

F_3_1_b_site_assessment.Priority form: For habitat types 6210, 7130, 9430 priority depends on the habitat characteristics. Indicated as Yes/No.
F_3_1_b_site_assessment.Significance: Indicates if the occurrence is significant or not.
F_3_1_b_site_assessment.Representativity: Degree of representativity of the habitat type on the site: A = excellent; B = good; C = significant
F_3_1_b_site_assessment.Relative surface: Area of the site covered by the habitat type in relation to the total area covered by that habitat type within the national territory, assigned to following percentage classes: A1 = 75-100%; A2 = 50-75%; A3 = 25-50%; A4 = 15-25%; B = 2-15%; C = smaller than 2%
F_3_1_b_site_assessment.Conservation: Degree of conservation: A = excellent (nearly all of the habitat area in good condition); B = good (most of the habitat area in good condition); C = reduced (most of the habitat area in not good condition); X = unknown (most or all of the habitat area in unknown condition).
F_3_1_b_site_assessment.Conservation area: Area of conservation degree categories in good (A + B), not good (C), unknown (X) conditions as hectare values.
F_3_1_b_site_assessment.Conservation method: Method used for degree of conservation: complete = Complete survey or statistically robust estimate in hectares (for example taken from mapping in management plans); limited = Based mainly on extrapolation from a limited amount of data (expert judgement); veryLimited = Based mainly on expert opinion with very limited data (based on partial mapping data); insufficient = Insufficient or no data available
F_3_1_b_site_assessment.Conservation objectives: prevent = Prevent deterioration; maintain = Maintain the habitat type's surface area and its good condition; enlarge = Enlarge the area of the habitat type; improve = Improve the habitat type condition; reestablish = Re-establish the habitat type; other = Other
F_3_1_b_site_assessment.Global: Global assessment of the habitat type in the site: A = excellent; B = good; C = significant.
F_3_1_b_site_assessment.Update date: Date or period of the last update.

F_3_2_a_essential_information.Group: The taxonomic group to which the species belongs: A = Amphibians; B = Birds; F = Fish; Fu = Fungi; I = Invertebrates; L = Lichens; M = Mammals; P = Plants including bryophytes and algae; R = Reptiles
F_3_2_a_essential_information.Sensitive: Species indicated with “true” are classified as sensitive.
F_3_2_a_essential_information.Not present: Indicates whether the species is no longer present (noLongerPresent) on the site, or its re-establishment is planned (reEstablishment).
F_3_2_a_essential_information.Type: Population type of species in the site: p = Permanent: to be found throughout the year on the site (non-migratory species, plants, resident population of migratory species); r = Reproducing: uses the site to raise young (e.g. breeding, nesting); c = Concentration: site used for staging or roosting or migration stop/over or for moulting outside the breeding grounds and excluding wintering; w = Wintering: uses the site during the winter
F_3_2_a_essential_information.Unit: The unit of population size values, the standard is i = individuals, p = pairs. For exceptions see reference portal.
F_3_2_a_essential_information.Abundance: Abundance category to be provided if no number of population size is available: C = Common; R = Rare, V = Very rare; P = Present
F_3_2_a_essential_information.Method population size: Method used for population size: complete = complete survey or a statistically robust estimate; limited = based mainly on extrapolation from a limited amount of data; veryLimited = Based mainly on expert opinion with very limited data; insufficient = insufficient or no data available
F_3_2_a_essential_information.Last data collection: Date or period of the last data collection.

F_3_2_b_site_assessment.Group: The taxonomic group to which the species belongs: A = Amphibians; B = Birds; F = Fish; Fu = Fungi; I = Invertebrates; L = Lichens; M = Mammals; P = Plants including bryophytes and algae; R = Reptiles
F_3_2_b_site_assessment.Significance: Indicates if the occurrence is significant or not.
F_3_2_b_site_assessment.SPA classification: Indicate if the bird species met the ornithological criteria used to justify SPA classification.
F_3_2_b_site_assessment.Population: Size and density of the population of the species present on the site in relation to the populations present within national territory, assigned to following percentage classes: A1 = 75-100%; A2 = 50-75%; A3 = 25-50%; A4 = 15-25%; B = 2-15%; C = smaller than 2%
F_3_2_b_site_assessment.Conservation: Degree of conservation: A = excellent (nearly all of the habitat occupied by the species has sufficient quality); B = good (most of the habitat occupied by the species has sufficient quality); C = reduced (most of the habitat occupied by the species has non-sufficient quality); X = unknown (most of the habitat occupied by the species has unknown quality).
F_3_2_b_site_assessment.Conservation objectives: prevent = Prevent deterioration; maintain = Maintain the extent and good quality of the habitat of the species and the population size; enlarge = Enlarge area of the habitat of the species; improve = Improve the quality of the habitat of the species (considering also disturbance and mortality factors); reestablish = Re-establish habitat for the species; increase = Increase the population size; reduce = Reduce pressure on the population (e.g. reduce mortality or disturbance); reestablishPopulation = Re-establish the population at the site; other = Other
F_3_2_b_site_assessment.Isolation: Degree of isolation: A = population (almost) isolated, B = population not-isolated, but on the margins of are of distribution, C = population not-isolated within extended distribution range
F_3_2_b_site_assessment.Global: Global assessment of the species in the site: A = excellent; B = good; C = significant
F_3_2_b_site_assessment.Update date: Date or period of the last update

F_3_3_other_species.Group: The taxonomic group to which the species belongs: A = Amphibians; B = Birds; F = Fish; Fu = Fungi; I = Invertebrates; L = Lichens; M = Mammals; P = Plants including bryophytes and algae; R = Reptiles
F_3_3_other_species.Code: For Birds, Annex II, IV and V species the code from the official code list as provided in the reference portal.
F_3_3_other_species.Sensitive: For Birds, Annex II, IV and V species the code from the official code list as provided in the reference portal.
F_3_3_other_species.Not present: Indicates whether the species is no longer present (noLongerPresent) on the site.
F_3_3_other_species.Unit: The unit of population size values, the standard is i = individuals, p = pairs. For exceptions see reference portal.
F_3_3_other_species.Abundance: Abundance category to be provided if no number of population size is available: C = Common; R = Rare, V = Very rare; P = Present

F_3_3_8_species_motivation.Motivation: The motivation for listing additional species: H.D. Annex II = Species of Annex II Habitats Directive in SPA; H.D. Annex IV = Species of Annex IV Habitats Directive; H.D. Annex V = Species of Annex V Habitats Directive; B.D. Annex I = Bird species of Annex I Birds Directive in a pSCI, SCI, SAC; Migratory birds = Migratory bird species in a pSCI, SCI, SAC; CFP prohib. = Prohibited species of Annex I of the Technical Measures Regulation under the common fisheries policy (EU Regulation 2019/1241); Int. conv. = Species listed/protected under international Conventions; H.D. Annex I = Typical species of Annex I habitat types; CWR/FRG = Crop Wild Relatives (CWR) / Forest Genetic Resources (FGR); IAS Union = Invasive alien species of Union concern (EU Regulation 1143/2014 on invasive alien species)

F_4_3_pressures.Pressure code: The code according to the pressure list from the Natura 2000 reference portal.
F_4_3_pressures.Rank: Relative importance of a pressure in the categories high, medium, low.

F_5_2_2_management_list.text: Reference and validity of the management plan(s)

F_5_3_1_measures_list.text: Necessary conservation measures are described in the following document(s)
